full_prompt: Design one representative manual rigid-frame adult wheelchair frame (cross-tube or rigid-fold, depending on design choice) compliant with ISO 7176-8:2014 (static, impact, and fatigue strength) for a nominal occupant mass of 100 kg (ISO wheelchair test dummy). The frame must be a single welded or bolted tubular aluminum assembly (typical 6061-T6 aluminum, 25-32 mm OD seat tubes, 1.5-2.5 mm wall), supporting a 450 mm wide x 400 mm deep seat, 4 wheels (two 610 mm / 24 inch rear drive wheels + two 200 mm / 8 inch front casters), armrests, footrest(s), and a backrest, total assembly mass target <= 15 kg. The frame must pass three independent ISO 7176-8 evaluations: (A) STATIC: 1.25 times the nominal downward seat load (i.e. 1.25 * 100 kg * 9.81 m/s^2 = 1226 N) applied through the seat dummy test plate; 1.25 x nominal on backrest, armrest, and footrest per the clause-specific static pad locations; permanent deformation must not exceed the clause limits and the frame must not fracture. (B) IMPACT: 6666 equivalent curb-drop impacts from 50 mm height per the double-drum / curb-drop rig with the 100 kg dummy installed; no fracture or loss of function (ISO 7176-8 curb-drop cycle count). (C) FATIGUE: 200,000 cycles on the two-drum fatigue machine with 100 kg dummy at the prescribed drum geometry; no fracture or loss of function. Target material is 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, E = 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m3). Deliverable is a single CAD assembly of the frame (tubes + welded gussets + wheel/axle mounts + caster forks + seat/back + armrest + footrest) suitable for linear-static structural FEA under ISO 7176-8 clause-specific static loads, explicit-dynamics curb-drop impact, and fatigue life evaluation to 200000 + 6666 cycles. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required. Explicit-dynamics impact solver and fatigue solver are mandatory non-FEA-static solvers.
short_prompt:
Design an adult manual rigid-frame wheelchair (welded/bolted tubular aluminum 6061-T6, 450 x 400 mm seat, 610 mm rear wheels + 200 mm casters, armrests, footrest, backrest) qualified to ISO 7176-8:2014 for a 100 kg ISO test dummy. Total assembly mass <= 15 kg.

Envelope: frame tubes 25-32 mm OD x 1.5-2.5 mm wall; geometry otherwise per the designer.

Load cases (ISO 7176-8 clauses).
- LC1 seat static: 1.25 x nominal = 1226 N through the seat test pad.
- LC2 backrest static: 1.25 x nominal at the backrest pad.
- LC3 armrest/footrest static: 1.25 x nominal at each clause-specific pad.
- LC4 curb drop: 6666 drops from 50 mm height with 100 kg dummy, no fracture or loss of function.
- LC5 double-drum fatigue: 200,000 cycles on the two-drum rig with 100 kg dummy, no fracture.

Curb-drop impact needs an explicit-dynamics solver; double-drum needs a fatigue-life solver.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Curb-drop 50 mm impact from 6666 drops requires transient dynamics with large-displacement contact (not linear static FEA).
200000-cycle double-drum fatigue cannot be evaluated with a single static FEA run.

prompt.title: Adult manual wheelchair frame qualified to ISO 7176-8
prompt.brief: Design a rigid manual wheelchair frame for a 100 kg ISO test dummy passing static (1.25 x nominal), impact (6666 x 50 mm drops), and fatigue (200000 double-drum cycles) per ISO 7176-8.

boundary_conditions.wheel_contacts: rear wheel and caster contact points provide vertical + tangential reaction
boundary_conditions.static_tests: hard floor under wheels; loads applied via ISO 7176-8 test pads

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield of 6061-T6; no permanent deformation beyond ISO 7176-8 clause limits

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam + shell for tubes, solid for welded gussets, rigid dummy
